cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the question is asking between what times Thomas could have gone to the football field. The answer options are given, and we have to determine which one is correct based on the information provided. First, let's list out all the details given: - Thomas woke up at 8am. - David saw Thomas fixing their computer at the electronic store from 8am to 10am. - Jennifer saw Thomas buying lunch at the deli from 10am to 12pm. - Lisa saw Thomas walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 12pm to 2pm. - Sarah saw Thomas buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 5pm. - Kimberly saw Thomas buying a bike at the bike shop from 6pm to 9pm. - The football field was closed after 9pm. So the football field is closed after 9pm, so Thomas must have gone there before 9pm. But we need to find the time when he wasn't doing any of the other activities. Let me map out Thomas's timeline based on the people who saw him: - 8am to 10am: at electronic store fixing computer (David) - 10am to 12pm: buying lunch at deli (Jennifer) - 12pm to 2pm: walking towards Statue of Liberty (Lisa) - 2pm to 5pm: buying phone at electronics store (Sarah) - 6pm to 9pm: buying bike at bike shop (Kimberly) So he woke up at 8am, and after that, he was at the electronic store from 8am to 10am. Then the timeline is full from 8am onward with various activities. Let's check for gaps. Between 8am and 10am: electronic store. Then 10am-12pm: deli. 12pm-2pm: Statue of Liberty. 2pm-5pm: electronics store. Then between 5pm and 6pm, there's no activity mentioned. Then from 6pm-9pm: bike shop. So the only time when Thomas's whereabouts aren't accounted for is between 5pm and 6pm. After that, he was at the bike shop from 6pm to 9pm. Before that, he was at other places. Therefore, the only possible time he could have gone to the football field is between 5pm and 6pm. Also, since the football field is closed after 9pm, he would have had to go before that, which is already covered by the answer.
